  "findings": [
    "Pool pump motor is not bonded to the equipotential bonding grid.",
    "Missing bonding connection creates risk of potential difference and electric shock drowning or injury.",
    "All metal components within 5 feet of pool edge, including pump motors, must be bonded together per NEC Art. 680."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "De-energize power to the pump motor and related pool equipment immediately.",
    "Install an 8 AWG solid copper bonding conductor from the pump motor to the common bonding grid.",
    "Verify continuity between all bonded metal parts and the common bonding grid.",
    "Inspect and bond all other required metal parts within 5 feet of the pool (handrails, ladders, conduit, enclosures, etc.).",
    "Do not energize or place pool equipment in service until bonding is verified and inspected."
  ],
  "rationale": "An unbonded pump motor in a pool/spa installation violates NEC Art. 680 equipotential bonding requirements and presents a critical shock/electrocution hazard due to possible potential differences in and around the water.",
  "references": [
    "NEC Art. 680"
  ]
}
